description_long: This object consists of two medieval-style swords, each featuring a straight, double-edged steel blade with a pointed tip. The blades are long and slender, designed for thrusting and slashing, and have a metallic sheen. Each sword has a simple crossguard that extends horizontally, providing hand protection, and a cylindrical handle with a dark brown coloration, likely made of wood or a similar material. The handles are capped with a rounded metallic pommel, adding balance and a classic aesthetic. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible engravings or decorative elements, emphasizing function over ornamentation. These swords would be suitable for display, theatrical use, or as props in an urban or historical context.
description: A pair of straight, double-edged swords with metallic blades, simple crossguards, and dark cylindrical handles capped with round pommels. The design is functional and unadorned.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad faces of the blades, crossguards, and rounded pommels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thinness and length of the blades, as well as the cylindrical handles and crossguards.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the blades and the pommels, similar to the front but with less detail on the blade faces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the slim profile of the blades and the alignment of the handles and guards.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, dark brown, capped with a round metallic pommel, smooth surface
